data.output:
/tmp/story-12-final.md
---
# DDx Story 12 — Optimize the prompts used to operate `ddx run` (final)

## Scope clarification

`ddx run` is a thin pass-through (`service_run.go:114`, `run_internal.go`) —
no DDx-owned system prompt. The static prompts DDx ships on every attempt and
that the user actually means to optimize are:

1. `executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` — execute_bead.go:1208 — ~1078 words / ~1400 tok
2. `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`  — execute_bead.go:1274 — ~1020 words / ~1330 tok
3. `beadReviewInstructions`            — execute_bead_review.go:124 — ~283 words / ~370 tok (LEAVE)
4. `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`   — execute_bead.go:1357 — ~42 words (LEAVE)

Selector at execute_bead.go:1343 routes `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` to the Agent
variant; everything else (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, unknown) uses the
Claude variant. This grouping is intentional — see plan-2026-04-18 §112 and
the portable-prompt principle (frame/principles.md:152). Story 12 keeps the
two-variant split.

## Diagnosis

### Load-bearing guardrails (every one MUST survive any rewrite)

Codex audit caught the original list missing several. Final list:

- AC checkbox is satisfied by a specific code/test/file (anti-handwave) — execute_bead.go:1208,1234
- Read first / read named files / read referenced specs before editing — execute_bead.go:1232,1300
- Missing-governing fallback note — execute_bead.go:1357
- Commit subject ends with `[<bead-id>]` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Commit exactly once, when green — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- `git add <specific-paths>`, never `git add -A` — execute_bead.go:1238,1308
- Do not commit red code — execute_bead.go:1236,1306
- Do not modify files outside bead scope — execute_bead.go:1261
- Never run `ddx init` — execute_bead.go:1260,1334
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — execute_bead.go:1259,1333
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md unless asked — execute_bead.go:1262,1335
- Bead description overrides CLAUDE.md / YAGNI defaults — execute_bead.go:1248,1328
- Reports go under `{{.AttemptDir}}/`, never `/tmp`, and are committed alongside code — execute_bead.go:1244,1318
- Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` before exiting empty — execute_bead.go:1240,1314
- Step 0 size-check + decomposition path — execute_bead.go:1210,1278
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do NOT declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open — execute_bead.go:1254,1324 (Agent variant has stronger phrasing)
- Stop after the commit succeeds (Agent post-commit runaway guard) — execute_bead.go:1310
- Agent-only: use tool calls, not `bash: cat`/`rg`/`ls` — execute_bead.go:1304
- Reviewer JSON fence + anti-echo rules — execute_bead_review.go:132–151

### Redundancy across the two execute-bead variants

Step 0 size check, "investigation reports under {{.AttemptDir}}/", "review
gate / `<review-findings>`", "bead overrides project defaults", and most of
the constraints tail are duplicated almost verbatim. Source-level dedup will
remove ~250 words of repeated text. Per-attempt dedup (after tightening)
targets ~30%+ word reduction in the rendered prompt.

### Note on prompt caching (codex catch)

`buildPrompt` at execute_bead.go:1392–1461 places dynamic bead metadata FIRST
and static instructions LAST. That means Anthropic's prefix-cache cannot help
at all today — every attempt cache-misses on the static suffix. Reordering
to put static instructions first would unlock cache hits across attempts
with the same prompt version. **Capture this as a follow-up bead** (B5) but
do not block Story 12 on it; reordering changes XML semantics and merits
its own change with separate review.

## Spec changes

### FEAT-022 amendment

Add under Functional / Shared evidence primitives:

> **Static-prompt minimum-prompt rule.** Every static instruction string DDx
> embeds in an LLM prompt MUST declare its load-bearing guardrails inline
> as a Go comment list above the constant, one bullet per guardrail with a
> pointer to the failure mode it prevents. Edits MUST preserve every listed
> guardrail. New guardrails MUST be added to the list. A regression test
> MUST assert each listed guardrail still appears in the rendered prompt.
> This rule applies to STATIC prompt bodies; dynamic evidence egress is
> covered by the §5–§7 cap primitives and is unaffected.

The closing sentence is per codex's correction: do not let this amendment
imply static-comment guardrails replace the existing dynamic-egress caps.

### New TD: prompt versioning + offline comparison + rendered-size report

Captures the measurement roadmap. Summary:

- Add `prompt_sha` (and later `prompt_version`) to `manifest.json` so
  attempt outcomes slice cleanly across rewrites using existing telemetry
  (usage.json, session_index.go session tokens, resolver_feat008.go
  efficacy view).
- Add a deterministic test fixture that renders both variants for a
  representative bead and reports byte / word counts, gated as a
  CI artifact (catches accidental bloat).
- Optional: `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=<name>` selector for offline A/B.

## Implementation plan

1. **B1 — Extract shared blocks (refactor only).**
   - Pull duplicated text into named constants:
     `instrStep0SizeCheck`, `instrInvestigationReports`,
     `instrReviewGate`, `instrBeadOverride`, `instrCoreConstraints`.
   - Both variants compose: per-harness preamble + per-harness process body
     + shared blocks.
   - Rendered prompt MUST be byte-identical (or trivially equivalent —
     whitespace only) to the current output. All existing tests pass
     unchanged.

2. **B2 — Tighten shared blocks for density.**
   - Step 0: prose → 4-bullet checklist + 4-step recipe (~120 → ~80 w).
   - Process: drop verification-command examples (the bead names them);
     keep "do not commit red code" as one bullet; keep `[<bead-id>]`,
     `git add <specific-paths>`, "commit exactly once", "stop after
     commit", "do not modify outside bead scope" as separate bullets
     (codex catch — must remain explicit). (~150 → ~85 w)
   - Review-gate: two bullets ("review is a gate"; "address every BLOCKING
     `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking
     findings open"). (~80 → ~45 w)
   - Bead-override: one sentence. (~70 → ~25 w)
   - Constraints tail: bullets, including `.ddx/executions/` intact and
     "never `ddx init`" as separate items. (~60 → ~40 w)
   - Add the load-bearing-guardrail comment block above each constant per
     FEAT-022 amendment.

3. **B3 — Render-size report + `prompt_sha` in manifest (lightweight v1
   measurement).**
   - Test fixture renders representative-bead prompt for each
     (variant, contextBudget) and prints word / byte count to a CI artifact.
   - `manifest.json` gains `prompt_sha` (sha256 of the rendered prompt).
     Existing analytics in resolver_feat008.go can group attempts by
     prompt_sha for before/after comparison. Codex insisted on shipping
     measurement in v1, not deferring entirely.

4. **B4 — Amend FEAT-022 + author TD.**
   - FEAT-022 minimum-prompt rule paragraph.
   - New TD captures `prompt_version`, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector, and
     the offline-comparison roadmap (NOT implemented in this story).

5. **B5 — (deferred / optional) Reorder buildPrompt to put static
   instructions first for prefix-cache compatibility.** Not on Story 12
   critical path; file the bead, do not work it here.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC1: After B1, both variants share ≥4 named instruction-block constants;
  no shared block text is duplicated across variants in source.
- AC2: After B2, rendered prompt for the representative bead is ≥30% shorter
  (word count) than current for both Claude and Agent variants.
- AC3: New test `TestExecuteBeadInstructionsLoadBearingGuardrails` asserts
  every guardrail listed in the file-level comment block (final list above)
  appears in the rendered prompt for each (harness, variant). Includes
  selector tests: `agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` → Agent; `claude`/`codex`/
  `opencode`/unknown → Claude (per codex catch).
- AC4: New invariant tests assert: rendered prompts contain
  `[<bead-id>]`, `git add <specific-paths>`, no `git add -A`,
  `no_changes_rationale.txt`, `.ddx/executions/`, `ddx bead create`,
  `ddx bead dep add`, `ddx bead update` (per codex catch).
- AC5: New test asserts non-minimal prompts include
  `executeBeadMissingGoverningText`; `contextBudget == "minimal"` omits
  governing refs as today.
- AC6: All existing prompt tests pass unchanged
  (`execute_bead_evidence_path_test.go`, `review_verdict_test.go`,
  `execute_bead_failure_mode_test.go`).
- AC7: `manifest.json` contains `prompt_sha` for every attempt; size-report
  fixture emits word/byte counts per (variant, contextBudget).
- AC8: FEAT-022 amended; new TD authored.
- AC9: `beadReviewInstructions` is unchanged. (NOT "out of scope" — the
  schema/fence guardrails are load-bearing and dynamic-evidence cost is
  already addressed by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`. Per codex.)
- AC10: No new per-harness branches beyond Claude/Agent.

## Tests (consolidated)

- Existing: `go test ./cli/internal/agent/...` passes unchanged.
- New: AC3 guardrail-list test.
- New: AC3 selector test.
- New: AC4 substring-invariant tests.
- New: AC5 missing-governing test.
- New: AC2 size-floor test (word count threshold per variant).
- New: AC9 reviewer-prompt regression test (fenced-JSON requirement, no
  prose, no template echo) — explicit re-assertion.

## Risks (post-codex)

- Guardrail loss — mitigated by FEAT-022 comment-list contract + AC3 test.
- Story 10 collision on Step 0 — serialize. (See coordination.)
- Per-harness drift — shared blocks; per-variant only owns preamble +
  process body.
- Cheap-tier behavior change on shorter prompts — `prompt_sha` in manifest
  + size-report fixture (B3) lets us slice efficacy/no_evidence_produced
  across the rewrite. If the metric worsens, revert is one constant edit.
- FEAT-022 amendment phrasing must NOT imply static-comment guardrails
  replace dynamic-egress caps (codex catch).
- `beadReviewInstructions` left alone is correct; reviewer's real cost is
  diff inlining, already bounded by `BuildReviewPromptBounded`.
- Prompt caching unaddressed in Story 12; B5 captured separately.

## Bead breakdown (final, with codex's split on B4)

- **B1 — Extract shared instruction blocks (refactor, byte-equivalent
  output).** Tier: cheap. Deps: Story 10 prompt edits merged.
  AC: AC1, AC6.

commit efbed403d81810fd410ebefb0f3399a8a72cb773
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 08:02:57 2026 -0400

    feat(agent): add prompt_sha to manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]
    
    Story 12 B3: lightweight v1 measurement for the execute-bead prompt
    optimization roadmap.
    
    - manifest.json gains prompt_sha (sha256 hex of rendered prompt bytes)
      so analytics in resolver_feat008.go can group attempts by prompt to
      compare outcomes across rewrites.
    - New deterministic test fixture renders both variants (Claude harness,
      Agent harness) at each contextBudget (full and minimal) and writes a
      word/byte report to DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT (default t.TempDir()).
      Asserts minimal < full bytes per variant to catch cheap-tier
      regressions.
    - CI integration job runs the fixture and uploads the report as the
      prompt-size-report artifact.

data.text: The implementation for AC7 already landed in commit `efbed403` (now on main) and tests pass. No additional code changes are needed; I'll record this as a no-changes attempt with rationale.

input.content:
Bead ddx-bffb23ae (Story 12 B3) — no_changes rationale
========================================================

Status: AC7 already satisfied on main; no further code change needed in
this attempt.

What is done
------------
A prior attempt landed commit efbed403 ("feat(agent): add prompt_sha to
manifest + prompt-size CI fixture [ddx-bffb23ae]") which is an ancestor
of HEAD/main in this worktree. Verified with `git merge-base HEAD
efbed403` → efbed403. That commit delivers every AC7 deliverable:

1. prompt_sha in manifest.json for every attempt
   - Field defined: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:177-181
     (`PromptSHA string \`json:"prompt_sha,omitempty"\``)
   - Populated on write: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1127
     (`PromptSHA: promptSHA(promptContent)`)
   - Helper: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1467-1470
     (`promptSHA` returns hex sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes)
   - Regression test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
     :230-298 asserts manifest.prompt_sha is a 64-char hex digest and
     equals sha256 of the prompt.md bytes on disk.

2. Render-size CI fixture emitting word/byte counts per (variant,
   contextBudget)
   - Test: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_promptsize_test.go
     (`TestPromptSizeReport`) renders both variants
     (claude harness, agent harness) at contextBudget = "" (full) and
     "minimal", logs the table to test output, writes a tab-separated
     human report and a JSON sibling, and asserts minimal < full bytes
     per variant to catch cheap-tier regressions.
   - Determinism guard: `TestPromptSHA_DeterministicAcrossRenders`
     asserts the helper is stable across renders and differs across
     variants.

3. CI surfaces the report as an artifact
   - .github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214 runs the fixture with
     `DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT=${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report
     .txt` and uploads `prompt-size-report.{txt,json,log}` as the
     `prompt-size-report` artifact.

Verification run in this worktree
---------------------------------
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run
'TestPromptSizeReport|TestPromptSHA|ExecuteBeadArtifacts' -count=1` →
ok (0.012s).

Why the bead was re-queued
--------------------------
The notes section on this bead reads "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only
this attempt's own execution evidence ... The shown manifest.json itself
lacks a prompt_sha field, contradicting AC7." That review fired against
a *different* attempt whose diff happened to contain only the evidence
files (manifest.json/result.json) for that attempt's own execution
bundle. The actual implementation landed on a separate commit
(efbed403) which is now on main. The recorded manifest.json the
reviewer inspected was generated by an *earlier* execution that ran
before efbed403 was merged, so it predates the field — the manifest.json
emitted by the current binary does include prompt_sha.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
Nothing for AC7. If the reviewer still doubts the field is being
written, point them at the fresh manifest.json in this attempt's
evidence directory (.ddx/executions/20260502T121152-24b051ff/
manifest.json) and the unit test
TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_ManifestPromptSHA in execute_bead_artifacts_
test.go which asserts prompt_sha is present and matches sha256 of
prompt.md.

data.text:
AC7 is already satisfied on main via commit `efbed403`:
- `prompt_sha` field added to manifest.json (`execute_bead.go:177-181, :1127`) with regression test in `execute_bead_artifacts_test.go:230-298`
- Render-size fixture `TestPromptSizeReport` in `execute_bead_promptsize_test.go` emits word/byte counts per (variant, contextBudget) and asserts minimal < full
- CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml:201-214`) runs the fixture and uploads `prompt-size-report` as an artifact

Tests pass in this worktree. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining that the prior REVIEW:BLOCK looked at a stale manifest from a pre-efbed403 run; current binary writes the field.
